core_finding: Across multiple CRC Visium datasets, spatially explicit domain methods show higher spatial coherence and cross-sample marker coherence than expression-only clustering baselines (with reported stability distributions).
semantic_boundary: Limit conclusions to the evaluated datasets, preprocessing, and K-grid; report per-sample heterogeneity and uncertainty (distributions/CI) rather than single-point “wins”. BayesSpace-gated comparisons are restricted to the BayesSpace-compatible cohorts (flat-matrix inputs; currently `GSE267401` and `GSE311294`), while baseline coverage includes all downloaded cohorts.
forbidden_implications: Do NOT claim “ground-truth tissue compartments”; do NOT claim universal superiority across all tissues/platforms; do NOT imply clinical utility or prognosis impact.

core_finding: Under a prespecified multiseed auditing suite, BayesSpace stability varies across samples and K settings; we report stability distributions and identify configurations that fall below the prespecified stability gates.
semantic_boundary: Only claim robustness/sensitivity for the declared perturbations (K-grid, seed scheme, and declared controls); always present sensitivity curves and failure cases. Stability and sensitivity conclusions are restricted to BayesSpace-compatible cohorts used for multiseed evaluation.
forbidden_implications: Do NOT claim “optimal K” as a biological truth; do NOT hide instability by selecting a single best seed; do NOT tune parameters based on downstream evaluation metrics post hoc.

core_finding: A CPU-friendly method subset can be run on an 8GB laptop for a local-first pilot; deep/GPU-heavy methods require cloud. We quantify runtime/memory and provide a practical decision guide.
semantic_boundary: Restrict compute claims to the measured hardware/software and declared timeouts; report wall time distributions and explicit failures. Compute feasibility is treated as scope-limited guidance rather than a universal method ranking.
forbidden_implications: Do NOT imply “fast = best”; do NOT claim cloud methods are inferior if not run; do NOT understate compute costs (must report failures/timeouts).
